install.note: ZeroClaw is a Rust binary installed via cargo. After install, verify with: zeroclaw --version

configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically via ~/.echobird/zeroclaw.json → ~/.zeroclaw/config.toml. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

first_run.description: ZeroClaw requires a TOML config file at ~/.zeroclaw/config.toml. ALL four fields are required: api_key, default_model, default_provider, default_temperature. Missing any field will cause startup errors.

required_fields.api_key: (string) API key for the LLM provider
required_fields.default_provider: (string) Provider URL with 'custom:' prefix, e.g. 'custom:https://api.deepseek.com/v1'. IMPORTANT: most OpenAI-compatible APIs need the /v1 suffix.

common_mistakes: WRONG: omitting default_temperature (ZeroClaw will fail to start)
WRONG: using JSON format (ZeroClaw uses TOML, not JSON)

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Map to ZeroClaw config.toml as follows:
field_mapping.attachment_baseUrl: → default_provider (prefix with 'custom:' and append '/v1' if needed)

steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, apiKey.
2. Create ~/.zeroclaw/ directory if it does not exist: mkdir -p ~/.zeroclaw
3. Run configure merge_script below with the extracted values.
4. Verify: zeroclaw agent -m 'say hello' (should print a greeting and exit cleanly)
configure.merge_script:
python3 -c "
import os

# --- fill from '[Attached models for deployment]' block ---
MODEL_ID   = '<attachment model field>'   # e.g. deepseek-chat
BASE_URL   = '<attachment baseUrl>'        # e.g. https://api.deepseek.com
API_KEY    = '<attachment apiKey>'
# ----------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure /v1 suffix for OpenAI-compatible APIs
if not BASE_URL.rstrip('/').endswith('/v1'):
    BASE_URL = BASE_URL.rstrip('/') + '/v1'

path = os.path.expanduser('~/.zeroclaw/config.toml')
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)

toml = f'''api_key = \"{API_KEY}\"
default_model = \"{MODEL_ID}\"
default_provider = \"custom:{BASE_URL}\"
default_temperature = 0.7
'''

with open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(toml)

print('Done:', MODEL_ID, '@', BASE_URL)
"

cli.description: ZeroClaw uses cli-oneshot protocol. Each message is a fresh invocation.
cli.note: ZeroClaw does NOT support JSON output mode or session resumption. Output includes ANSI color codes and log lines that must be cleaned.
